title: Leucanthemum sylvaticum
taxonomy: Leucanthemum sylvaticum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). Within this family, it belongs to the subfamily Asteroideae and tribe Anthemideae. The genus Leucanthemum is further divided into the subgenus Chrysopsidium and the subgenus Leucanthemum. The species Leucanthemum sylvaticum is also known as the woodland daisy or wood meadow daisy. Its classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies.
uses: Leucanthemum sylvaticum, also known as Woodland Oxeye Daisy, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. However, there is limited information on its specific medicinal uses. Traditionally, the plant has been used in various folk remedies, often for its perceived antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. More research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption. Its medicinal uses are not widely recognized or studied in modern medicine.
benefits: Leucanthemum sylvaticum is a flowering plant also known as the Woodland Oxeye Daisy. Although not extensively studied, Leucanthemum sylvaticum is believed to offer some health benefits. Its flowers and leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The plant may also have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in wound healing and prevent infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the exact health benefits of Leucanthemum sylvaticum.
properties: Leucanthemum sylvaticum is a wildflower that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The plant is thought to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. Its leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids, which are believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Leucanthemum sylvaticum has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. More research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic properties of Leucanthemum sylvaticum.
constituents: Leucanthemum sylvaticum contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils as its active constituents. The flavonoids present in the plant, including flavonols and flavanones, have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, the plant's phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have been found to possess antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. The essential oils of Leucanthemum sylvaticum have been shown to have a soothing effect on the skin and mucous membranes. These active constituents contribute to the plant's traditional uses in herbal medicine, particularly for treating skin and respiratory issues.
parts: Leucanthemum sylvaticum, also known as woodland daisy, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. preparations: Leucanthemum sylvaticum is a plant species that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The roots and leaves of this plant have been used in various preparations for their potential health benefits. In some traditional herbal practices, the plant's extracts are used to treat fever, cough, and digestive issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of these preparations. intro: Leucanthemum sylvaticum, commonly known as the wood meadow daisy, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The wood meadow daisy has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Leucanthemum sylvaticum include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
